programming_answers:
def merge_and_sort(arr1, arr2):
    combined = arr1 + arr2
    combined.sort()
    return combined
session1_transcript:
[00:00:15] Interviewer_Technical_Senior: Welcome Aisha. You scored 5/5 on MCQs covering decision trees, regularization, quantization, RAG context windows, and activation math. Walk us through your coding approach.
[00:00:35] Candidate: For the first question I used list concatenation and Python's native Timsort via .sort() which runs at O(N log N). For the second question the bug was a type mismatch — list primitives passed into code expecting hash-set traits. I resolved it by converting to a set for O(1) lookups then back to a list.
[00:01:10] Interviewer_Technical_Junior: Can you break down your MLOps stack?
[00:01:25] Candidate: I deploy models across Azure ML and AWS EC2. I use MLflow for experiment tracking and model registry, GitHub Actions for CI/CD pipelines, and Prometheus with Grafana for monitoring throughput and latency anomalies.
[00:02:10] Interviewer_Technical_Senior: How does context management adapt when moving from text RAG to multi-modal retrieval?
[00:02:30] Candidate: Visual data is far denser than text tokens so you need asymmetric embedding structures — extracting visual features into a unified vector space using models like CLIP and matching them with textual summaries. For text you use sliding windows or re-ranking schemas.
[00:03:15] Interviewer_Technical_Junior: Why did you choose LangGraph over standard LangChain for your NLP-to-SQL project?
[00:03:40] Candidate: LangChain operates as linear chains which fail when SQL validation fails and needs a self-correcting loop. LangGraph uses stateful nodes and conditional edges — when a validation node catches a failure, the edge routes back into a correction node. I backed this with AWS RDS and ElastiCache for Redis caching of schema discoveries.
session2_transcript:
[00:00:10] Interviewer_HR: What would you consider your core professional strengths and weaknesses?
[00:00:25] Candidate: My strength is absorbing new engineering frameworks rapidly and deploying production-ready configurations. My weakness is diving too deep into low-level performance details during prototyping — I am actively managing this by setting strict timeboxes for discovery phases before writing code.
[00:01:10] Interviewer_HR: Are you comfortable with both long-term and fast-paced delivery cycles?
[00:01:30] Candidate: Yes. Short deadlines push me to ship minimal viable vertical slices. Longer timelines let me engineer robust infrastructure and eliminate technical debt. I rely on structural decomposition when deployment windows narrow.
[00:02:15] Interviewer_HR: Where do you see yourself in five years?
[00:02:30] Candidate: Advancing into a Senior AI Architect role designing orchestrated multi-agent systems and enterprise MLOps pipelines where agent intelligence drives operational efficiency.
